<activity
    android:name=".YourActivity"
    android:label="@string/title_activity_your_activity">
    <intent-filter>
        <action android:name="android.intent.action.SEND" />
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
        <data android:mimeType="text/plain" />
    </intent-filter>
</activity>

In the fixed code, the `<intent-filter>` is added within the `<activity>` tag in the AndroidManifest.xml file. The `<intent-filter>` specifies that the activity can handle the `ACTION_SEND` action, accepts data of type `text/plain`, and is a default activity for handling such intents.

By defining the intent filter correctly in the manifest file, other apps can discover and launch your activity when an implicit intent is sent with the `ACTION_SEND` action and the data type is `text/plain`. This allows your app to respond to sharing intents from other apps.://

- `<data>`: Specifies the MIME type of the data being sent, in this case, `text/plain`, indicating that the activity can handle plain text data.
